title: BUG: harden JSON datetime/timedelta serialization against crashes
description:
closes #65903
closes #65904
closes #65905

Hardens the C-level JSON serialization helpers against NULL returns and a use-after-free:

- `int64ToIso` (`date_conversions.c`): when `make_iso_8601_datetime` fails, `result` was freed but execution fell through to `strlen(result)` / `return result` — a use-after-free. Added the missing `return NULL;`, matching the existing pattern in the sibling `int64ToIsoDuration` (GH-65903).
- `get_long_attr` (`objToJSON.c`): NULL-check `PyObject_GetAttrString` before dereferencing, consistent with the existing `_creso` error handling in the same function (GH-65904).
- `total_seconds` (`objToJSON.c`): NULL-check `PyObject_CallMethod` and switch the unsafe `PyFloat_AS_DOUBLE` macro to the safe `PyFloat_AsDouble` (GH-65905).

All three code paths are guarded upstream by type checks (`PyObject_HasAttrString(..., "_value")` / `PyDelta_Check`), so I couldn't construct an input that actually triggers a crash on `main` — these are defensive hardening fixes, hence no regression test.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

review_comments.target_code:
@@ -361,6 +361,7 @@ I/O
 - Bug in :meth:`DataFrame.to_stata` raising ``KeyError`` when column names require renaming and ``convert_dates`` is specified for a different column (:issue:`60536`)
 - Bug in :meth:`DataFrame.to_string` where ``formatters`` dict was applied to wrong columns when output was horizontally truncated via ``max_cols`` (:issue:`35410`)
 - Fixed :func:`read_json` with ``lines=True`` and ``nrows=0`` to return an empty DataFrame (:issue:`64025`)
+- Hardened :meth:`DataFrame.to_json` and :meth:`Series.to_json` against potential crashes (a use-after-free and NULL-pointer dereferences) when serializing datetime and timedelta objects (:issue:`65903`, :issue:`65904`, :issue:`65905`)
